stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: ‘Our patience is not endless’ – Kyiv signals peace offer may expire
stories.summary: Full and unconditional ceasefire is a major compromise that Ukraine might ‘recalibrate and modify’, says UN envoy. What we know on day 1,581 Ukraine may revise its ceasefire offer to Russia if the UN security council fails to pass a resolution urging a full and ⁠unconditional end to ⁠the ​hostilities, Kyiv’s envoy to the UN has warned. Ukraine had changed ​the dynamic in the war with recent strikes, said Andrii Melnyk, adding that some 40% of Russia’s oil refineries had been damaged . Melnyk tol

stories.title: Trump spent a decade making friends in Europe. Now they’re turning away.
stories.summary: PARIS — For Europe’s populist right, U.S. President Donald Trump’s embrace was once seen as a political asset. Not anymore. For years, nationalist leaders across the continent treated the American president’s support as proof that their politics had gone global. But with major elections looming in 2027, including in Italy, France and Poland, many are rethinking the value of that transatlantic backing. Trump’s brand in Europe has soured — curdled by his tariff wars, threats against Greenlan

stories.title: Whistleblower investigating Ecuadorian president’s family business was murdered, activists say
stories.summary: Monika Silva Koniuszek died from a blow to the head and strangulation, a postmortem found, despite government claim of suicide Campaigners in Ecuador say a Polish anti-corruption activist who investigated allegations against the family business of the country’s rightwing president was murdered to silence her. Monika Silva Koniuszek, 41, was found dead in her home in Montañita, a coastal town in Ecuador’s Santa Elena province. The single mother of daughters aged four and nine, was found on the fl

stories.title: US Senate passes bipartisan bill to lower housing costs
stories.summary: 21st Century Road to Housing Act, which aims to boost supply and stop investors buying up homes, heads to House Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox The Senate on Monday passed a bipartisan measure aimed at lowering housing costs by streamlining construction and permitting, ending months of fraught negotiations on a priority for both parties ahead of November’s midterm elections. The 21st Century Road to Housing Act would limit investors’ ability to buy h

stories.title: The Guardian view on the death of Carlo Ginzburg: a historian who taught us to think about outsiders | Editorial
stories.summary: The work of one of Italy’s greatest scholars focused on ordinary lives oppressed by power and prejudice. That approach resonates today Reflecting on the genesis of his most famous work, Carlo Ginzburg wrote that by immersing himself in the trial of a 16th-century miller burned by the Roman Inquisition, he turned a possible footnote into a book. Fifty years on, after being translated around the world, The Cheese and The Worms still stands as a supreme exemplar of historical research devoted to th

stories.title: How 100 hospitals switched to pen and paper to defeat a national cyber-attack
stories.summary: For four days, dozens of Romanian hospitals went offline, as cyber-experts sought to defeat the hackers.

stories.title: 44 embassies and cultural institutes express support for Budapest Pride
stories.summary: The foreign representations welcome the new government’s affirmation that Hungary will be home for every Hungarian and that everyone can feel they have a place in the Hungarian nation.

stories.title: Weather tracker: Extreme heat in Europe as typhoon strengthens off Philippines
stories.summary: Eastern Europe to see temperatures above early July average into next week The extreme heat experienced across central and western Europe, including the UK, will continue to shift eastwards. As slightly cooler weather infiltrates into western Europe, with risks of downpours and thunderstorms, eastern Europe is likely to see temperatures several degrees above the early July average into next week. Peak highs of between 35C and 40C are expected across southern Poland, Slovakia, Hungary and into th

stories.title: A magyar pártok egymásra licitálva migrációs paktumoznak, de miről szól ez az egész?
stories.summary: A parlamenti pártok egymást túlkiabálva igyekeznek ellenezni az uniós menekültügyi csomagot, a kormány pedig jelezte, hogy a mostani formájában nem hajtják végre a migrációs paktumot. Magyar az EU-ban úgy látta, sokan mások is elégedetlenek a szöveggel, noha számos szigorítást tartalmaz. Azt is megnéztük, hogy jön ide a naponta egymillió euró, amit Magyarország elbukik.

stories.title: Lakner Zoltán: Szent László a melegeké is, mert mi is magyarok vagyunk
stories.summary: A politikai elemző a Pride-körüli parlamenti vitára reflektált – kezdve azzal, hogy a felmérések alapján a társadalom kétharmada támogatná az azonos nemű párok örökbefogadási jogát.

stories.title: Az Amnesty Magyarország Sulyok eltávolításáról: A kormány mostani megoldása nincs rendben
stories.summary: A szervezet kifogásolta, hogy a kormány csak öt napot adott a véleményezésre, szerintük a megfosztási eljárást kellene alkalmazni.

stories.summary: Az ellenőrzés már előző este elkezdődik és másnap reggelig tart.

stories.summary: Még a főmegvesztegető is elismerte, hogy a Főváros vezetését nem lehet megvesztegetni, reagált a főpolgármester a Telex cikkére.

stories.summary: Mbappé szerint fizikailag és mentálisan is megterhelő volt közel két órán keresztül úgy várakozni az öltözőben, hogy közben folyamatosan készen kellett állniuk a játék folytatására.

stories.summary: Egy negatív rekord is becsúszott az argentin klasszisnak.

stories.title: Bóka Jánosnak az alkotmányos puccs, a csengőfrász és Rákosi Mátyás jut eszébe a Tisza kormányzásáról
stories.summary: Fortélyos félelmet is emlegetett napirend előtti felszólalásában. „Ez egy élmény volt” – reagált beszédére Magyar Péter. Kedden törvényhozási nagyüzem lesz, a szavazások előtt Magyar Péter a V4-es csúcs apropóján beszélt, Toroczkai László megalkotta a „liberálglobalizmus” fogalmát, amit aztán ígérete ellenére se fejtett ki. Élőben a Parlamentből.

stories.title: Megbukott a román államfő miniszterelnök-jelöltje, újraindul a kormánykeresés Bukarestben

stories.title: Navracsics Tiborék nyílt levélben kérik a jobboldali tőkéseket a fideszes média finanszírozására
stories.summary: Azt írják, hogy egyes nagytőkések hatalmas vagyonra tettek szert, és a viselkedésük miatt a jobboldali sajtó munkatársainak is tartaniuk kellett a hátukat. Emiatt most kötelességük segíteni.

stories.summary: A humorista-író egyszerre öt karaktert alakít a rendszerváltóknak címzett „rendkívüli, fogalomtisztázó” különkiadásban.

stories.title: Új híd épülhet a lakossági és a vállalati digitális ügyintézés között (x)
stories.summary: A magyar vállalatok digitális működésében egyre nagyobb szerepet kapnak azok a megoldások, amik nemcsak gyorsabbá, hanem jogilag is biztonságosabbá teszik az üzleti folyamatokat. A Digitális Állampolgárság Programhoz kapcsolódó szabályozási környezet, valamint az AVDH gazdálkodó szervezeteket érintő 2025. novemberi kivezetése új helyzetet teremtett: a céges elektronikus ügyintézésben és nyilatkozattételben felértékelődött a piaci alapú, minősített elektronikus aláírás szerepe.

stories.title: Orbánék tétlenkedése miatt összezsugorodtak a napelemes és egészségügyi célok, Magyarék a zöld közlekedésre gyúrnak
stories.summary: Nyilvánossá vált a 10 milliárd eurót érő uniós helyreállítási alaphoz beadott magyar terv jóváhagyott módosítása. Ennek beadására azért volt szüksége a Tisza-kormánynak, mert az Orbán-kormány rosszul haladt a saját vállalásaival. Megnéztük, mi változott.

stories.summary: A műtők egy részében nem biztosíthatók folyamatosan a megkövetelt feltételek.

stories.title: Russian dissident artist Semyon Skrepezki murdered in Poland
stories.summary: Skrepezki was known for his scathing caricatures of Russian President Vladimir Putin. His suspected killer has been apprehended.

stories.title: Czech government causes alarm with plan to scrap license fee

stories.title: Andy Burnham will inherit a kingdom disunited by Brexit
stories.summary: LONDON — Everyone says that Britain isn’t supposed to work like this, that the chaos of chewing through six prime ministers in a decade is just “not normal” for one of the most stable and ancient democracies in the world.  But what if it is?  As Andy Burnham prepares in all likelihood to become the seventh person in 10 years tasked with forming a government, he stands to inherit a Labour party full of inexperienced MPs who have — sometimes literally — grown up in the era of Brexit poli

stories.title: Cities must set the terms for the AI infrastructure boom
stories.summary: The digital economy we all take for granted runs on infrastructure most people never see. Every message sent, every doctor’s appointment booked, every journey mapped and payment made depends on vast networks of data centers working around the clock. Across the C40 network of nearly 100 global cities alone, there are already more than 1,700 data centers, with many more planned. stories.title: A fire in LA has been burning for days. What’s taking so long to put it out?
stories.summary: While warehouse fires are often extinguished in a day, the Boyle Heights blaze is on its sixth day. Here’s what to know Los Angeles firefighters are on their sixth day of battling a fire at a massive warehouse near downtown that stores frozen food. Smoke has billowed from the warehouse, which was covered in solar panels and insulated like a freezer, filling the air surrounding the roughly 500,000-sq-ft (46,450-sq-meter) facility. The 230-page memoir was written almost 80 years ago by Kiyoshi Tanimoto, who witnessed the city’s destruction after the atomic bomb was dropped in 1945. stories.title: Peter Murrell jailed for five years after embezzling £400,000 from SNP
stories.summary: Estranged husband of Nicola Sturgeon is sentenced for stealing from party over 12-year period Peter Murrell has been sentenced to five years and three months in jail after he admitted embezzling more than £400,000 from the Scottish National party while he was its chief executive. stories.title: Canadian healthcare staff decry ‘cruel hoax’ after scam email promises paid day off
stories.summary: Unions condemn ‘insensitive’ internal cybersecurity test sent to healthcare workers in Newfoundland and Labrador For years, healthcare staff in the Canadian province of Newfoundland and Labrador have felt overworked and underappreciated. Turnover, burnout and thinning resources were pushing workers in the sector to a breaking point. So when the email titled “June Holiday” arrived in thousands of inboxes, they felt a moment of overdue joy. Continue reading...

stories.title: Montreal mayor calls for end to random police checks amid racial profiling investigation
stories.summary: City’s police force faces investigation of 16 officers accused of disproportionately targeting Black and Arab residents Montreal’s mayor has called for a halt to random police checks as the city’s police force grapples with an internal investigation into racism and racial profiling by 16 officers. Mayor Soraya Martinez Ferrada told reporters last week that her husband, who is Black, had been repeatedly stopped by police while driving. Continue reading...

stories.title: US: Trump threatens prison for Reflecting Pool 'vandalism'
stories.summary: The Trump administration spent $14.7 million repainting the pool "American flag blue" ahead of US' 250th anniversary celebrations. Weeks later, the paint is peeling off and algae has turned the water green.

stories.title: Israel, Lebanon to hold new round of US-hosted talks
stories.summary: Israel and Lebanon prepare for a fresh round of negotiations in Washington. Iran says negotiating groups to oversee sanctions, nuclear issues, economic reconstruction and implementation. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Germany's pension plans draw praise and outrage
stories.summary: A 33-point plan to reform Germany's pension system has drawn praise and criticism in almost equal measure. Centrist politicians have called it a good compromise, but opposition parties on the left and right are unhappy.

stories.title: More than a unibrow: The enduring appeal of Frida Kahlo
stories.summary: She shunned feminine beauty ideals, was bisexual, politically active and brutally honest in her art — all while being ahead of her time. A major Frida Kahlo exhibition opens at London's Tate Modern.

stories.title: US waives Iran oil sanctions citing progress in talks
stories.summary: US officials have cited ongoing negotiations on allowing nuclear inspections and opening the Strait of Hormuz in pausing sanctions for 60 days. JD Vance said a "foundation" is in place for a final deal.

stories.title: Why is Bulgaria threatening to veto EU's Russia sanctions?
stories.summary: Bulgarian PM Rumen Radev has vowed to veto the EU's latest sanctions against Russia because they include Russian Orthodox Patriarch Kirill and former Lukoil president Vagit Alekperov. What's behind his opposition?

stories.title: A brutal gang rape in India revives painful memories of 2012 Delhi assault
stories.summary: The case from Begusarai in Bihar comes close in brutality to that inflicted on a woman during a sexual assault in 2012.

stories.title: Vance says direct talks with Iran set 'good foundation' for deal to end the war
stories.summary: Lower-level officials are continuing to engage in what officials described as “technical talks” in Switzerland following Vance, Ghalibaf and Araghchi’s departure, as the US and Iran look to inch closer to a concrete deal.

stories.title: Kazakhstan brings New Silk Road revival to Brussels as Tokayev visits EU leaders
stories.summary: Kazakhstan's president has arrived in Brussels this week to meet EU leaders and assess the delivery of promises made at last year's EU-Central Asia summit in Samarkand, with the Middle Corridor trade route connecting China to Europe through Central Asia at the top of the agenda.

stories.title: Why was JD Vance in a luxury Swiss resort for talks with Iran?
stories.summary: BBC correspondent Jessica Parker explains some key points of the direct talks between the US vice-president and Iran.

stories.title: Iran says no new commitments on nuclear sites after Vance says inspectors to be invited back
stories.summary: Iran's foreign ministry says it made "no new commitments" on nuclear inspections after talks in Switzerland.

stories.title: Nancy Guthrie ransom note said she died after abduction
stories.summary: Guthrie went missing from her home in Tucson, Arizona, in early February. The FBI is offering a reward up to $100,000 for information leading to her location.

stories.title: Inquiry ordered after building fire kills 15 in north India city
stories.summary: Witnesses described people jumping from windows to escape, while rescue teams broke through a wall to save lives.

stories.title: Brussels residents cool off in fountains as heatwave grips Belgium
stories.summary: People flocked to fountains in Brussels' Parc du Cinquantenaire as temperatures climbed during a heatwave expected to intensify later this week.

stories.title: Police officer and civilian killed in Montreal shooting
stories.summary: Montreal Police named the officer killed in the shooting as 34-year-old Mohamed Lamine Benredouane.

stories.title: Watch: French jets salute US anniversary with Washington flyover
stories.summary: France's elite Patrouille de France aerobatic team performed a ceremonial flyover above some of Washington's most iconic sites as part of celebrations marking 250 years of American independence.

stories.title: Dettol apologises after ad to clean up 'toxic men' backfires in China
stories.summary: The ad sparked accusations of sexism in China, after featuring a man looking for a partner who is "not tainted by other men".

stories.title: Ransom note claims Nancy Guthrie died after abduction
stories.summary: The note from the possible kidnappers reportedly stated that they did not mean for her to die and included an apology to the family.

stories.title: Alpine glacier melts faster as second heatwave hits France
stories.summary: The Bossons Glacier in France’s Chamonix Valley is showing fresh signs of rapid retreat as a second heatwave of 2026 grips parts of Europe.

stories.title: Montreal shooting leaves officer, civilian and suspect dead
stories.summary: It was the first time in 24 years that a Montreal police officer was killed in the line of duty, the city's police chief said.

stories.title: US-backed political newcomer wins Colombia election, marking country's right-wing shift
stories.summary: With nearly all votes counted, the election results polarised the country, sparking both celebrations and protests across the country.

stories.title: At least 13 killed and dozens injured after Qatar gas explosion
stories.summary: The city's main liquified natural gas (LNG) processing site suffered a "technical accident" in the Ras Laffan industrial zone.

stories.title: Myanmar army killed over 700 civilians in six months, UN says
stories.summary: The new UN report says the 702 civilian deaths over six months last year included 153 children.

stories.title: Kenya minister found in contempt of court over US-backed Ebola centre
stories.summary: Aden Duale defied a High Court order to stop building work on a controversial US-funded facility.

stories.title: Former Kenyan justice minister blocked from entering Uganda, lawyers' body says
stories.summary: Martha Karua is a lawyer representing detained Ugandan opposition leader Kizza Besigye who is on trial for treason.

stories.title: Saving costs and improving lives
Germany's Left Party is dreaming big
stories.summary: The socialist Left Party has seen its membership soar and is especially popular with young female voters. Could it soon come to power in the city state of Berlin?

stories.title: Former US Fed chair Alan Greenspan dies aged 100
stories.summary: Alan Greenspan oversaw the US Federal Reserve for nearly two decades from 1987 until 2006. Critics argue that his policies contributed to the Global Financial Crisis of 2008.

stories.title: The man who knew, but didn't say: Alan Greenspan (1926-2026)
stories.summary: Alan Greenspan’s reputation as head of the US Federal Reserve was legendary — not least because of his reputation for convoluted statements. However, the 2008/9 financial crisis took a toll on his standing.

stories.title: 75 years of Germany's Goethe-Institut
stories.summary: For 75 years, the Goethe-Institut has stood for cultural exchange, education and partnerships around the globe — even under challenging political conditions.

stories.title: How inflation undermines Nigeria's sugar tax
stories.summary: Nigeria's sugary drinks tax was meant to stop rising diabetes rates, but soaring inflation has eroded its impact. Experts say the levy is too small to change behavior and urge higher rates and reforms.

stories.title: US-Iran deal: Pakistan's diplomatic masterstroke?
stories.summary: By stepping in as mediator in the Iran war, Pakistan has managed to elevate its status from a regional player to a global diplomatic stakeholder — with a little help from China and its friends in the Gulf.

stories.title: Iran-US talks to end war beset by tension, mistrust
stories.summary: While both sides claim "encouraging progress," experts say there's a long way to go to turn the shaky diplomatic framework into a durable agreement.

stories.title: Germany and UK rebuilding ties 10 years after Brexit vote
stories.summary: Ten years after the referendum, the two countries are strengthening their ties once again. The stability of these ties is now being put to the test with Keir Starmer to be replaced as prime minister.

stories.title: Europe sizzles under heat wave with little relief in sight
stories.summary: Germany, France, the UK and other European countries are experiencing extreme heat, with cooler weather not expected until the weekend. Several deaths were reported in France, including two children. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Meet Andy Burnham, Britain's likely next prime minister
stories.summary: Now that British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation, all eyes are on Andy Burnham. The former mayor of Manchester is seen as the favorite to succeed Starmer to lead the United Kingdom.

stories.title: World Cup: Amiri another wildcard for Nagelsmann's Germany
stories.summary: The Mainz midfielder with Afghan roots has become a key member of Germany's 2026 World Cup squad. Coach Julian Nagelsmann said Amiri can provide the team with a spark when it's most needed.

stories.title: Late singer Oliver Tree's body returned to US after helicopter crash
stories.summary: The 32-year-old California native died whilst on his world tour, ahead of a date in Brazil.

stories.title: Alan Greenspan, architect of the modern American economy, dies aged 100
stories.summary: As chairman of the Federal Reserve, Alan Greenspan became the world's most high-profile banker.

stories.title: Canal Saint-Martin becomes cooling spot as heatwave grips Paris
stories.summary: Paris faces extreme heat as crowds use fountains and the Canal Saint-Martin to cool off, while authorities impose alerts and safety measures.

stories.title: Giant dragon and octopus kites fill Danish skies
stories.summary: Thousands gather at Denmark's Fano International Kite Festival as giant dragon, whale and octopus kites fill the skies above the coast.

stories.title: Iran and US make progress on Hormuz despite Trump's explosive threats
stories.summary: JD Vance said 18 hours of talks in Switzerland had "laid a very good foundation" after both sides agreed a mechanism to keep the Strait of Hormuz open and maintain Lebanon's ceasefire, even as Trump threatened to hit Iran "only harder".

stories.title: WhatsApp to be led by Indian start-up founder as Will Cathcart steps back
stories.summary: WhatsApp's leadership shake-up comes as Meta looks to strengthen its app's already booming presence in India.

stories.title: Twins marry twins in joyous Nigerian joint wedding
stories.summary: Brothers Taiwo and Kehinde Oguntoye speak to the BBC after getting hitched to twin sisters.

stories.title: 'Another day, another leader': Londoners react to Starmer's resignation
stories.summary: People in central London expressed frustration, uncertainty and cautious hope after Keir Starmer announced his resignation as UK prime minister.

stories.title: Red heat alerts issued in France, Italy and Spain as 40C temperatures forecast
stories.summary: The heatwave conditions are forecast to intensify in the coming days across central and western Europe.

stories.title: Japan to increase visa fees by more than five times starting on 1 July
stories.summary: Japan wants to help manage the rising costs of its immigration system by making tourist visas significantly more expensive in the first fee increase in nearly half a century.

stories.title: Messi smashes all-time World Cup goalscoring record with 18 goals for Argentina
stories.summary: Messi has become the highest-scoring player in World Cup history with 18 goals. He now has two more than the previous record holder, former Germany striker Miroslav Klose.

stories.title: Belgium issues visas to Taliban delegation for EU migration talks
stories.summary: The EU is preparing to host Taliban representatives for the first time since the group's return to power in Afghanistan.

stories.title: Honduras to buy Ukrainian drones to fight drug trafficking
stories.summary: The discussions come as the Central American country continues to grapple with drug trafficking and some of the highest levels of violence in the region.

stories.title: Norvégia–Szenegál 3-2
NER-es és külföldi cégek nyerhetnek az óriásplakátok fővárosi eltüntetésén
stories.summary: A Fővárosi Önkormányzat szerint végre nincs politikai akadálya a városképi és közlekedésbiztonsági szempontok érvényesítésének, a végén mégis az eddigi nagy szereplők pozíciója válhat még erősebbé.

stories.title: Haaland duplázott Szenegál ellen, továbbjutottak a norvégok
stories.summary: A szorosnak tűnő végeredmény nem is volt annyira szoros, a 93. percben született az afrikai csapat szépítő gólja.

Az államosított lombikbébiszektornak is hirdetéseket gyártottak Balásy Gyula cégei
stories.summary: 86,5 millió forintot használtak fel a 211 millió forintos keretösszegből, hogy hirdessenek valamit, aminek nem is igazán kell reklám. A közbeszerzést a Nemzeti Kommunikációs Hivatal írta ki, az eljárás végén Balásy cége szerződhetett az Országos Kórházi Főigazgatósággal.

stories.title: Az ausztrál opálparadicsom, ahol a lakók fele a föld alatt él
stories.summary: Coober Pedy igazi poros bányaváros, egészen addig, amíg az ember a felszín alá nem néz: a település fele ugyanis sziklába vájt otthonokban lakik, de a helyi templomok és az iskola is a föld alatt található.

stories.title: Mbappé a kétórásra nyúlt félidei viharszünet előtt és után is gólt rúgott Iraknak
stories.summary: Egy vihar miatt két órán keresztül állt a játék, a háromgólos győzelmet végül Dembélé biztosította be a franciáknak.

stories.summary: Iszonyú sok közpénz ment el a TSM-rendszerre, aminek fenntartása is gigantikus összeg. Egyetlen bökkenő van: nem használható arra, amire létrehozták. Az előző kormány a kezdeti blama után nem mert hozzányúlni, pedig nagyon gyorsan megtérülne, így a mostani vezetés számára ígéretes terep a kijavítása.

stories.title: A Győri ETO játékosa gólt szerzett a Jordánia ellen fordító algériai válogatottban

stories.title: Egyre csak fokozódik a kánikula, a hét második felében akár 38 fok is lehet
stories.summary: Elfogynak a hűsítő zivatarok,sőt, még felhőt is csak nagyítóval fogunk találni az ország fölött.

stories.title: Egy év alatt 21 ezer embert rúgtak ki az Oracle-től a mesterséges intelligencia miatt
stories.summary: A létszámleépítés a későbbiekben is folytatódhat az amerikai techóriásnál.

stories.summary: A norvég kormányzat szabályozza az MI-használatot, és szeretnék, ha a papíralapú könyvekből is több lenne az iskolákban.

stories.summary: A volt képviselő szerint a törvénytervezettel véglegessé vált, hogy az új kormány nem számít az ő munkájára.

stories.summary: A jegybank azért szabott ki felügyeleti bírságot, mert a Mol késve tájékoztatott arról, mi a helyzet a Barátság kőolajvezetékkel, és ez hogy érinti a Molt.

stories.title: Bocsánatot kért rasszista kijelentése után a szerb tévés szakértő
stories.summary: Az 56 éves egykori csatár a Belgium–Irán mérkőzésen kiállított Nathan Ngoy kapcsán fogalmazott kifogásolható módon.

stories.summary: Az M1-esen kapcsolta le a kisteherautót a NAV, és a raktér ellenőrzésekor találták meg az elrejtett borítékot a több millió forint értékű régi érmékkel.

stories.title: J. D. Vance szerint Irán beleegyezett az atomfegyver-ellenőrök visszaengedésébe, Irán szerint nem vállaltak ilyesmit
stories.summary: Mindeközben az Egyesült Államok ideiglenesen felfüggesztette az Iránnal szembeni, évtizedek óta fennálló szankciókat.

stories.summary: A norvég huhogós evezés eddig a vb egyik legsikeresebb koreográfiája.

stories.title: Elfogadta a parlament a médiatörvényt, amivel felszámolják a jelenlegi közmédiát
stories.summary: Kitakarítják a kuratóriumokat, kettészedik az MTVA-t, lesz újra önálló nemzeti hírügynökség. stories.title: Nincs elég víztartalék Zalaszentgróton, II. fokú vízkorlátozást vezetnek be

stories.summary: Több mint száz oldalon taglalják a reformokat, amelyekkel a kormány hozzáférhetne szinte minden befagyasztott európai uniós forráshoz.

stories.summary: Hiába nincs még fizetésük, a bankok számára nagyon értékesek a diákok, hiszen a megszerzésük után akár évtizedekig az ügyfelek maradnak ugyanannál a pénzintézetnél. stories.summary: Ameddig kizárólag a korábban megszokott vízállás visszaállítása a cél, a problémák újra és újra fel fognak merülni, állítja Dr. Boromisza Zsombor, a MATE docense.

stories.title: ‘This changes everything’: how Brexit altered Scotland’s political landscape
stories.summary: Former party leaders reflect on the turbulence that followed the referendum in which most Scottish voters backed the losing side The decision to quit the EU bolstered support for Scottish independence, which a decade after the Brexit referendum is at near record levels, according to Scottish Labour’s former leader Kezia Dugdale. Dugdale said the Brexit vote “creates a frame around fairness” for many in Scotland because, unlike England, Scottish voters comprehensively backed remain in 2016, by 62

stories.title: Dutch PM apologises for Moluccan soldiers’ mistreatment after Indonesian independence
stories.summary: Rob Jetten acknowledges grief and pain of Moluccan families as crowdfunded monument unveiled in Rotterdam The Dutch prime minister, Rob Jetten, has formally apologised for the “heartless” mistreatment of thousands of Moluccan soldiers who fought for the Dutch colonial army during Indonesia’s struggle for independence. stories.title: Far-right millionaire wins Colombia’s razor-tight presidential election
stories.summary: Leftwing opponent alleges vote count irregularities after Trump-endorsed lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella secures narrow majority A Trump-admiring far-right millionaire lawyer and self-styled “outsider” has defeated a leftwing senator by a razor-thin margin to win Colombia’s presidential runoff, in an election that promises to mark a dramatic shift in the country’s decades-long armed conflict. With 99.99% of ballots counted in the preliminary vote tally, the far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Esprie

stories.title: US-Iran talks strained as Trump threats spark Iranian walkout
stories.summary: Talks expected to continue for rest of the week despite disruption caused by US president’s threat to bomb Iran and kidnap negotiating team High-stakes talks between the US and Iran are expected to continue for the rest of the week in Switzerland, after a tense start that saw Iranian negotiators walk out in protest at a stream of threats issued by Donald Trump on social media. The US president had threatened to bomb Iran and even to kidnap the Iranian negotiating team unless the strait of Hormuz

stories.title: UK's Starmer mulls 'political realities' after Burnham by-election
stories.summary: A member of UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer's Cabinet said it would be "delusional" to pretend there was no threat to his position. But he also said he had no information suggesting he might quit, as a newspaper reported.

stories.title: Scottish police arrest man over Edinburgh attacks
stories.summary: Five people were injured in the attacks on Friday that were described by Prime Minister Keir Starmer as being motivated by "anti-Muslim hatred."

stories.title: US and Iran publicly trade threats as talks take place in Switzerland
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump on Sunday threatened to strike Iran if it did not stop Hezbollah from "causing trouble", while Iran's chief negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf warned the US to "be careful with their statements".

stories.title: Currency crash and visa crackdowns force Indian students to rethink studying abroad
stories.summary: A weaker rupee and tougher immigration rules are pushing Indian students away from popular destinations like the US and UK.

stories.title: 13 killed and 66 injured in explosion at Qatar’s largest energy site, authorities confirm
stories.summary: Qatar Energy announced on Monday afternoon that a massive accidental explosion and blaze at the country’s main energy site at Ras Laffan the night before killed 13 people and injured 66, as workers tried to resume operations. Qatar says its LNG exports are not affected.

stories.title: Russian strikes on eastern Ukraine kill three and injure 22
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Saturday that his forces had targeted an oil-processing facility in Russia's Tyumen region.

Negotiations in Lucerne between the US and Iran have already run into difficulties, after Trump wrote on Truth Social that “Ir

stories.title: Brexit: how it has hit your wallet at the supermarket and on holiday
stories.summary: Ten years on, leaving the EU has made life more difficult and costly – here are some of the ways we’ve lost out It is 10 years since voters in the UK chose to leave the EU, and our wallets have been feeling the effects ever since. From paying more to take the dog on holidays in France – and making calls while you are there – to higher grocery bills and the headache of filling in customs forms for parcels, Brexit has made many simple tasks more complicated and expensive. stories.summary: Egy járókelő, egy rendőr és az elkövető vesztették életüket, egy rendőrnő megsebesült.

stories.title: Germany could scrap law banning insults against politicians

stories.title: Germany's media rocked by AI scandal
stories.summary: Two German outlets were forced to delete articles that used undisclosed AI. Many fear that reliance on the technology will damage the credibility of German media.

stories.title: How TikTok-style videos keep human brains hooked on content
stories.summary: TikTok, Instagram and YouTube clips are designed to overwhelm the brain's pleasure circuitry and keep people watching. Researchers say short-form videos may only be the start when it comes to harvesting human attention.

stories.title: ‘A flying White House’: President Trump unveils the new Air Force One
stories.summary: Converted from a former Qatari-owned Boeing 747, the new Air Force One will serve as a temporary presidential aircraft until Boeing delivers the long-delayed replacement fleet in 2028.

stories.title: Ethiopia PM's party wins landslide as fears grow of new conflict

stories.title: Parisians cool off in canal amid 'red alert' heatwave

stories.title: Is Germany looking again at coal-powered electricity?

stories.title: The BTS fans losing thousands as scammers cash in on comeback tour ticket war

stories.title: Russian troop build-up threatens city seen as key to seizing Ukraine's Donbas
stories.summary: If Kostyantynivka falls, Russian forces would be able push towards Ukraine's last remaining strongholds in the east.

stories.title: Trump says repairs to algae-plagued Reflecting Pool will begin immediately
stories.summary: Despite a multi-million dollar renovation, the Washington DC landmark has peeling paint and algae. Trump claims vandals are partly to blame.

stories.title: Largest ever cocaine bust in Australia after police raid underground bunker
stories.summary: Police seized 2.7 tonnes of cocaine worth an estimated A$816m after searching a property in western Sydney.

stories.title: Trump-backed political outsider wins Colombia election, initial count shows
stories.summary: Abelardo de la Espriella appears to have narrowly defeated his rival Iván Cepeda, who says the preliminary count is "not yet official or binding".

stories.title: Lower-level technical talks to continue as first round of direct US-Iran negotiations wrap up
stories.summary: Iran’s top diplomat hailed mediators Pakistan and Qatar’s efforts over what he called “major progress” to end the continued Israeli offensive on Lebanon, despite a fragile truce. The comments came after Iran and the US kickstarted a 6-day negotiating window to permanently end the war.

stories.title: Japan quintuples visa fees in first price hike since 1978
stories.summary: Authorities say they do not expect the hikes to have an "immediate impact on inbound tourism".

stories.title: Spain pound Saudi Arabia 4-0 as Egypt tops Group G in matchday two fixtures of the 2026 World Cup
stories.summary: Spain returns to its winning ways as Egypt break their group’s draw deadlock in the second matchday fixtures for groups G and H at the 2026 World Cup.

stories.title: Australian police seize nearly 3 tonnes of cocaine in what has become the largest bust on record
stories.summary: The bust of nearly 3 tonnes is the country’s largest ever and is believed to hold a street value of just shy of €500 million.

stories.title: Norway fans take over Times Square with Viking row
stories.summary: Norwegian supporters in Viking helmets and national colours packed Times Square, chanting and singing before Norway’s Group I match against Senegal.

stories.title: China announces sanctions on 10 US companies as trade tensions flare
stories.summary: The sanctions come a month after US President Donald Trump visited Beijing for talks with Chinese President Xi Jinping aimed at stabilising relations.

stories.title: Sweat, tears and camaraderie as 20,000 runners take on world’s largest ultramarathon
stories.summary: For one day every June, South Africa’s searing racial inequality seems to melt away at Comrades race In the early morning dark, thousands of runners waited, jostling with anticipation. South Africa’s national anthem rang out. Then the haunting swell of Shosholoza , first sung by Zimbabwean migrant workers in South Africa’s goldmines. Finally, that unmistakable, spine-tingling piano: Chariots of Fire. Runners gather before the start of the marathon Continue reading...

stories.title: Spain: PM's wife must face corruption trial, judge rules
stories.summary: A court in Madrid ruled that there is sufficient evidence against Begona Gomez, the wife of Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez. The 55-year-old university director is banned from leaving the country.

stories.title: Zelenskyy issues one-week ultimatum to Lukashenka over drone-guidance equipment
stories.summary: During a media briefing in Kyiv on Friday, Zelenskyy threatened Ukraine will take down the drone relay equipment on the border if the Belarusian leader, Aliaksandr Lukashenka, doesn't do anything to address it.

stories.title: Trump threatens Hormuz tolls as ships sail on despite IRGC's closure claim
stories.summary: Iran's IRGC said it had closed the Strait of Hormuz over Israeli strikes on Lebanon, but CENTCOM said 55 ships carrying 17 million barrels of oil transited unhindered. Trump meanwhile warned of US tolls if a final deal was not reached within 60 days.

stories.title: Colombia's escalating, brutal internal conflict is defining its presidential election
stories.summary: A left-wing senator who backs talks with armed gangs faces an outsider endorsed by Trump.

stories.title: US-Iran peace deal talks underway in Switzerland
stories.summary: "We're going to hopefully make progress on the nuclear issue, make progress on the Lebanon ceasefire issue," US Vice President JD Vance told reporters.

stories.title: Taliban order ban on smartphones as officials shown destroying devices
stories.summary: Directive aimed at government workers, but reports of wider implementation spark warnings of future Afghanistan-wide prohibition The Taliban have ordered a sweeping ban on the use of smartphones by government officials – in what some analysts say could foreshadow broader, population-level restrictions. In a directive issued by the Taliban’s military courts and reviewed by the Guardian, the ban was to take effect this week and prohibits “high rank, low rank, general mujahideen, or service staff”

stories.title: Inequality is elementary to Germany's school system
stories.summary: Children's paths through Germany's school system are often determined before their first day of kindergarten, according to the national report on education. An elementary school in Bonn is offering a new model for kids.

stories.title: France bans alcohol during Fete de la Musique amid heat wave
stories.summary: France is taking additional measures as millions of people gear up for Fete de la Musique under punishing temperatures.

stories.title: Are Ukraine drones really exposing gaps in Russia's defense?
stories.summary: Once seen as almost impenetrable, Russian air defenses are now under growing strain from Ukrainian drones. A recent attack on Moscow has reignited debate over Russian vulnerabilities.

stories.title: Terror police probe suspected anti-Muslim attacks in Edinburgh
stories.summary: Counterterrorism police have taken charge after a series of violent attacks in the Scottish capital that left five men injured.

stories.title: Undav heroics help Germany beat Ivory Coast
stories.summary: In Germany's second 2026 World Cup game, Deniz Undav helped Germany to a comeback win against Ivory Coast in Toronto.

stories.title: Italian tourist dies in Dominican Republic resort blaze
stories.summary: Around 1,690 tourists were evacuated from a luxury resort in the village of Bayahibe after a fire broke out.

stories.title: Steve Rosenberg: Moscow oil refinery attack brings Russia's war with Ukraine closer to home
stories.summary: There are moments when life in Moscow feels completely normal - Thursday morning wasn't one of them, writes Steve Rosenberg.

stories.title: BBC sees destroyed villages in Israeli-occupied southern Lebanon
stories.summary: Travelling with a humanitarian convoy, BBC's Hugo Bachega has been given rare access to a part of Lebanon under Israeli occupation.

stories.title: Meet Osito, the rescue dog winning hearts at the World Cup
stories.summary: A rescue dog named Osito has become an unlikely World Cup star in Mexico City, attracting crowds, selfies and social media attention wherever he goes.

stories.title: Why an AI company cleaned my New York City apartment for free
stories.summary: An AI company is sending free cleaners door-to-door in a bid to train the robots it hopes one day will replace them.

stories.title: At CrimeCon true crime obsessives come face-to-face with real loss
stories.summary: Victims' families say there are ways to be an ethical true-crime fan - not an exploitative one.

stories.title: Backstage at Gorillaz' epic, one-off stadium show: 'The vibe is ridiculous'
stories.summary: Damon Albarn, De La Soul and Moonchild Sannelly talk backstage as Gorillaz play their biggest show.

stories.title: Former Olympian denies vandalising Washington Reflecting Pool after arrest
stories.summary: Davey Hearn says he was simply touching the new paint at the site out of curiosity and did not remove or alter it.
